description: Maps common documentation claims to repository evidence. For Debrief/DCI tooling and human review.

claims.statement: The pattern engine defines exactly 18 rules in PATTERN_RULE_IDS.
claims.automated_check: scripts/verify_documentation.py imports PATTERN_RULE_IDS and asserts len == 18

claims.statement: The React client package.json exposes npm scripts: dev, build, preview (no npm start).
The API exposes POST /api/v1/assist/story-angles via routes/assist.py and main.py.
claims.automated_check: scripts/verify_documentation.py checks files and assist_router include

claims.statement: The test suite collects at least REGRESSION_FLOOR tests (CI enforces minimum passed).
claims.automated_check: scripts/verify_documentation.py runs pytest --collect-only and compares to floor

claims.statement: Primary languages are measured as JavaScript under client/ and Python as .py files, with vendor dirs excluded; counts and hashes live in docs/DEBRIEF_STRUCTURE_EVIDENCE.json.
The repository includes both client/ and server/ top-level directories; evidence uses marker file hashes and tree hashes.

note: Debrief DCI scores depend on which sentences are extracted from README/README fragments. This file lists claims that are fully verifiable in-repo. Hardcoded counts (e.g. 311 tests) in prose drift; prefer the verify script and pytest output for exact numbers.
